LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT STEVEN MILLER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. EVEN ASSUMING THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT RELY ON DEFENDANT'S UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION OF 3 DAYS NOTICE BECAUSE THAT CONDUCT IS PRIVILEGED, DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO CARRY HIS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT PROVE WRONGFUL EVICTION THROUGH DEFENDANT'S OTHER ALLEGED CONDUCT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR # 8189

MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON DEC-19-2007 IN 206, DEFENDANT STEVEN MILLER'S MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL AND/OR SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IS GRANTED. THE TRIAL DATE OF 1/7/08 IS CONTINUED TO 3/24/08 IN DEPT. 206 AT 9:30 AM. THE CURRENT SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE OF 12/19/07 IN DEPT. 604 IS MAINTAINED. DISCOVERY ACCORDING TO THE NEW TRIAL DATE. PARTIES STIPULATED TO HAVE MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION HEARD ON SHORTENED TIME AND HEARING MAY BE HEARD 15 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL WITH 30 DAYS NOTICE. OPPOSITION IS DUE IN 15 DAYS. 15 DAYS TO RESPOND TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES. WRITTEN ORDER PENDING. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON; CLERK: DLOK/EBURA; REPORTER: LETICIA PEREZ, CSR #7782(206)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. JAMES H. ODDIE AND MICHAEL R. BRACAMONTES OF BRADSHAW & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 433-4800 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BRADLEY J. JAMESON OF PHILLIPS SPALLAS & ANGSTADT LLP, TEL. NO. 278-9400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY STEVEN MILLER.

RULING - A R G U E D ; THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - DEFENDANT STEVEN MILLER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. EVEN ASSUMING THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT RELY ON DEFENDANT'S UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION OF 3 DAYS NOTICE BECAUSE THAT CONDUCT IS PRIVILEGED, DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO CARRY HIS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT PROVE WRONGFUL EVICTION THROUGH DEFENDANT'S OTHER ALLEGED CONDUCT. Ruling:
